title: K14448: Administrative commands to remove and rebuild ARX metadata

content.sfdetails__c:
Topic

Summary

The ARX system has administrative commands available to remove and rebuild the ARX metadata.

Description

The ARX system commands nsck ... destage and nsck ... rebuild are responsible for the removal and rebuilding of the ARX volumes metadata. You would typically use these commands when metadata corruption has reached a state that is beyond the ARX system's ability to repair the metadata. The ARX metadata is a database that the ARX system uses to track the location of files and directories on multiple disparate shares for ARX managed volumes.

When initiated, the nsck ... destage and nsck ... rebuild commands remove the ARX metadata from the external filer and release the back-end shares from a managed volume; the nsck ... rebuild command continues to initiate the re-import of the associated filers and shares.

The destaging and import of the ARX metadata are manual processes. These processes are highly disruptive, and you must consider external factors before completing a destage and import. F5 will not automate the initiation of the metadata removal or import processes.

Recommendations

Before initiating the rebuild of the ARX metadata, F5 recommends that you have identified and resolved the ARX system and infrastructure issues.

Supplemental Information

  • For information on using the nsck ... destage and nsck ... rebuild commands, refer to the ARX CLI reference guide.
  • For further information about the ARX metadata, refer to K9500: Overview of ARX metadata.
